body:
Audit source: 2026-06-11 residual census.

The census reports this open STIR proof-system residual:

- `StirIOP.MultiRound.stirMultiRoundRbrSoundnessResidual` — `ArkLib/ProofSystem/Stir/MultiRoundAssembly.lean:222`

This is an explicit `def : Prop` obligation rather than a raw axiom. It should be discharged from the single-round STIR soundness/completeness bricks plus the multi-round append/RBR composition infrastructure, or narrowed to the exact missing theorem.

Acceptance criteria:

- Connect the current STIR round soundness bricks to the multi-round assembly theorem.
- If blocked on generic append/RBR composition, reference and depend on #340 rather than duplicating that residual.
- Avoid replacing the residual with `True`, raw `axiom`, or a theorem-shaped placeholder.
- Add focused `#print axioms` checks for the promoted STIR theorem and keep the residual census open count decreasing.

comments.author: lalalune
comments.body: **Scoping (from the #301 assembly lane — I built this file): the census entry is the WRONG target.** `stirMultiRoundRbrSoundnessResidual` (`MultiRoundAssembly.lean:222`) is the **SHELL-verifier** residual — its docstring already carries the verdict: *LIKELY FALSE for sub-1 budgets* (the shell verifier is `verify = pure true`, accepts everything, so no nontrivial rbr budget is dischargeable). It is retained only as general wiring for `stirMultiRoundIOP_isSecureWithGap` and explicitly says *do not try to discharge it at a sub-1 budget*. **The genuine open obligations live on the CHECKING verifier** (`CheckingVerifier.lean`, whose 50-line honesty block is the authoritative map): `stirCheckingRbrSoundnessResidual` + `stirCheckingCABridge` — the bridge (Johnson-CA + per-round gap accounting ⟹ rbr-KS of the checking verifier) is the genuine open protocol-soundness math of #301, **gated on the #304 BCIKS20 correlated-agreement cores** (`StrictCoeffPolysResidual` family). The small-field/vacuous-regime routes are already discharged outright (`stirCheckingRbrSoundness_of_small_field` etc., no soundness residual consumed). **Recommendation**: re-point this issue at `stirCheckingCABridge` (large-field regime) and mark it dependent-on-#304; the shell residual should be excluded from the census as warning-documented wiring, same pattern as #340's statement-gapped residuals.

title: STIR: discharge multi-round RBR soundness residual
